sectiondef.header: PLL Register Offsets for Dynamic Line Rate Configuration

briefdescription.para: Number of PLL registers programmed per bucket.

memberdef.name: XMipi_Rx_Phy_PllRegOffsets
memberdef.initializer: = { XPLL_REG_2_M_OFFSET, XPLL_REG_3_M_OFFSET, XPLL_REG_4_O1_OFFSET, XPLL_REG_5_O1_OFFSET, XPLL_REG_6_O0_OFFSET, XPLL_REG_7_O0_OFFSET, XPLL_REG_22_D_OFFSET, XPLL_REG_23_D_OFFSET, }

briefdescription.para: PLL register index to offset mapping (used in programming loop):

detaileddescription.para: PllVals order: REG_2_M, REG_3_M, REG_4_O1, REG_5_O1, REG_6_O0, REG_7_O0, REG_22_D, REG_23_D

briefdescription.para: This function writes a value to a X5PLL register space.

detaileddescription.para: A 32 bit write is performed. If the component is implemented in a smaller width, only the least significant data is written.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the X5PLL instance.
is the register offset of the register to be written.
is the 32-bit value to write into the register.

briefdescription.para: This function programs the X5PLL registers for a given line rate.

detaileddescription.para: It looks up the matching bucket from the line rate table, then performs the PLL unlock -> configure -> re-lock sequence.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the X5PLL register space.
is the desired line rate in Mbps (400-4500 Mbps).

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if PLL programming is successful.
XST_FAILURE if no matching line rate bucket is found.
